game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Leps World Jump N Run Games** Players control Lep the leprechaun in a 2D side-scrolling platformer, navigating through 160 levels across 6 distinct world themes. Movement is controlled via a virtual D-pad on the left side of the screen, while a dedicated jump button on the right allows players to tap for a standard jump or hold for a higher leap. Lep's objective is to reach the flag at the end of each level, collecting scattered gold coins for score and cloverleaves to restore one of his three health points, while avoiding or defeating 9 unique enemy types by jumping on their heads. Levels progressively introduce more complex platforming challenges, increased enemy density, and environmental hazards like bottomless pits, with losing all health or falling into a pit resulting in a level restart. Successfully completing levels unlocks subsequent stages, and players can aim to collect all gold coins within a level to achieve a perfect score or unlock bonus content.
game_info.description: Guide Lep the leprechaun through a challenging platformer adventure! Navigate through levels filled with enemies, collect gold coins for points, and grab cloverleaves to restore health. Jump on enemies to defeat them, avoid falling into pits, and reach the flag at the end of each level to progress. You have 3 health points - lose them all or fall into a pit and you'll restart the level. Can you master all the worlds and collect every coin?
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←/→): Move left and right
Space: Jump (hold for higher jump)
Enter: Start the game from the start screen
Escape: Pause/unpause during gameplay
R: Restart from game over screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic player movement and physics. This test verifies that the player can move left and right using arrow keys, and that gravity and collision with the ground work correctly. It also checks that the player doesn't fall through platforms.
TEST_1.strategy_description: The test will alternate between moving left and right for short durations, allowing the physics engine to settle between movements. It will verify the player stays on the ground platform and responds to directional inputs appropriately.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should smoothly move left and right on the ground platform without falling through. The player's position should change in response to inputs, and gravity should keep them grounded when not jumping. The test succeeds if the player remains stable and responsive for at least 300 frames.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the complete win condition by reaching the flag. This test navigates the player through the first level, avoiding or defeating enemies, and reaches the flag to trigger the win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: The test will move the player right continuously with strategic jumps to avoid obstacles and enemies. It will jump when near enemies to potentially land on them, and maintain forward momentum to reach the level's end flag.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The player should successfully navigate to the flag at the end of the level, triggering the GAME_OVER_WIN state. The test succeeds when gameState.gamePhase changes to "GAME_OVER_WIN" within 600 frames, indicating successful level completion.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing jump mechanics including variable jump height. This test verifies that tapping space produces a short jump while holding space produces a higher jump, and that the player can only jump when on the ground.
TEST_3.strategy_description: The test will perform a series of short taps followed by longer holds of the space key, measuring the maximum height reached in each case. It will also attempt to jump while in mid-air to verify double-jumping is prevented.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Short taps should produce jumps of approximately 60-80 pixels in height, while held jumps should reach 120-150 pixels. Mid-air jump attempts should have no effect. The test succeeds if jump heights are consistently different and no double-jumping occurs over 200 frames.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing coin collection and score system. This test verifies that collecting gold coins increases the player's score and that coins disappear after collection.
TEST_4.strategy_description: The test will guide the player to intentionally collide with several coins placed in the level, monitoring the score counter after each collision. It will move systematically to collect at least 3-5 coins.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Each coin collected should increase the score (by 10 points per coin) and the coin should be removed from the game world. The test succeeds if the score increases correctly and collected coins no longer appear after 250 frames of collection attempts.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing enemy collision and health system. This test verifies that touching enemies from the side damages the player, while jumping on enemies defeats them.
TEST_5.strategy_description: The test will deliberately collide with an enemy from the side to take damage, then attempt to jump on another enemy's head to defeat it. It will monitor health changes and enemy removal from the game.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Side collisions should reduce health by 1 point. Jumping on enemies should defeat them and remove them from the world. The test succeeds if health decreases from side hits and enemies are removed when stomped within 300 frames.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing the lose condition by depleting all health. This test verifies that losing all 3 health points triggers the game over state.
TEST_6.strategy_description: The test will intentionally collide with enemies from the side repeatedly to take damage until all health is depleted. It will monitor the health counter and game phase transition.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: After taking 3 hits from enemies, the player's health should reach 0 and the game phase should change to "GAME_OVER_LOSE". The test succeeds if this transition occurs within 400 frames of starting the damage sequence.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing cloverleaf health restoration. This test verifies that collecting cloverleaves restores 1 health point and cannot exceed maximum health.
TEST_7.strategy_description: The test will first take damage from an enemy, then collect a cloverleaf to restore health. It will also attempt to collect a cloverleaf at full health to verify it doesn't exceed the maximum.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: After taking damage, collecting a cloverleaf should increase health by 1. At full health (3), collecting a cloverleaf should not increase health beyond 3. The test succeeds if health restoration works correctly and caps at maximum within 300 frames.
